title: asplenium trichomanes nothosubsp. malacitense
intro: Asplenium trichomanes nothosubsp. malacitense, also known as the Malacite spleenwort, is a rare subspecies of the Asplenium trichomanes fern, native to the Iberian Peninsula. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Asplenium trichomanes nothosubsp. malacitense include its ability to soothe coughs and relieve congestion, making it a popular remedy in traditional medicine. The plant can be prepared as a tea or infusion by steeping the leaves in hot water, or it can be dried and used in herbal supplements.
